Quotes, you know, fill page with piece of clip art. Doesn't matter. That's what I'm telling you. It's better than a book. And Brooks actually did more with this than I did, but both of us made a lot of money from this little book. I used this for years. You guys probably haven't seen some of these, have you? I'll bet there's 100,000 of these things out there. And all this is, is old columns and newsletter material and monologues and stuff put together in a book. And there's a lot of pictures and clip art and stuff, so it's filled it up, you know, with graphics and stuff. So, I mean, I was in a hurry, you know what I'm saying? Like, I always am. So this is like slapped together on a Saturday. But, I mean, it served its purpose. And a handful of these going out with stuff, now you can, you know, do better, and we got more sophisticated over the years. This book, by the way, this is one of my NSA positioning books. And here's how far this, let's see, when was this done? And here's how cheap I was. Let's see if there's a copyright in it. This is like 1981, introduction by very young looking Mark Victor Hansen. We were both, we both had hair. This book is typeset on the typewriter. For those of you who don't know, typewriter is a thing about a roller keys. This is typeset on the typewriter, okay? The bright side ain't justified, because I didn't want to take the time. I know how to do it on a typewriter. You got to type it twice and go back and take the units out and it's big, big, big ass. So I didn't just, I didn't even justify the right side. I mean, I typed it and we printed it. That's what, we put a cover on it, okay? This thing probably made me, I don't know, half a million bucks. There's a direct pitch in it for a course in the back, so a lot of courses, and essentially there's what you would call my brochure in the middle, resources, services, and so forth. And we just mailed this to every NSA member. That's what we did, okay? Better than a direct mail piece. This is a book I ghost wrote for a franchise or Don Dwyer, a carpet cleaning franchising, and instant elevation of him above all other competing franchise or is because he's the author of the book on how to be successful in business. So the franchise prospects getting the service master pitch, these getting like four or five, carpet cleaning franchise pitches, the only guy, the only franchise or is it's written a book on how to be successful as Don. Okay? Better thing to put in the package. So you gotta send them stuff that wows them. Big box of stuff. If you happen to have a lot of testimonials, so Stephen asked me how many books is right, because Stephen's got like 19 books, and some of them are like the Manhattan phone book. Like the book on place is like the Manhattan phone book. It's like big, heavy, thousand pages. Nobody's ever gonna read it. Nobody. There's a lot more entertaining. Oh yeah, it's really entertaining. Don't flatter yourself. It's a very good book, but entertaining it isn't. Painful would be a better description. Worth the pain, by the way, but painful. And nobody's ever gonna read it. He hadn't even read it. And nobody's gonna read all this stuff you send them. That's not the point. The point is you got more stuff than anybody else's got. So you gotta be a genius. If he sends all 19 of his books, believe me, the guy couldn't live long enough, even if he wanted to, to read all this stuff. But holy crap, look at all this stuff. Larry Dolan used to send, came out of LMI, a speaker consultant friend of mine, one of them big boxes that you get reams of paper in, like at Office Max now, you know, the big box that all the reams of paper come in, fill it up with his testimonial letters. Put duct tape around it and send it out to the prospect. Does anybody actually go to read all those testimonial letters? No, they don't have to. That's not the point. The point is the bulk in this case. Now they're gonna read a few of them, but after that it's like, enough already. Get it, you know, and the other four guys sent me a trifle brochure. So if you are good at cold prospect, wow them.
